title: ranunculus pedunculatus subsp. revelierei
intro: Ranunculus pedunculatus subsp. revelierei, also known as the Wood Buttercup, is a subspecies of the Ranunculus pedunculatus plant, a perennial herb native to Western Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for wound healing and skin conditions. The health benefits of Wood Buttercup are believed to include reducing inflammation, fighting off bacterial infections, and providing antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of Wood Buttercup typically involve drying the leaves and flowers, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal herb.
